spots.name: Victoria Peak
spots.shortDescription: Hong Kong Island’s best-known high viewpoint, with a sweeping perspective over Victoria Harbour and Kowloon.
spots.whyVisit: The historic Peak Tram, skyline panoramas and an easy scenic walk make it a first-time essential.
spots.bestTime: Late afternoon to evening for daylight, sunset and city lights
spots.openingHours: Peak Tram and venues have separate daily hours
spots.entranceFee: Peak viewing areas are free; Peak Tram fare applies
spots.topActivities: Peak Tram, Sky Terrace, Lugard Road walk, sunset photography
spots.nearbyAttractions: Central, Hong Kong Park, Lan Kwai Fong
spots.travelTips: Go on a weekday or arrive early; use the Circle Walk for wide views with fewer crowds.

spots.name: Victoria Harbour & Star Ferry
spots.location: Central and Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong
spots.shortDescription: The iconic harbour crossing links Hong Kong Island and Kowloon with close-up views of the city’s famous skyline.
spots.whyVisit: A short, inexpensive ferry ride delivers one of the world’s most memorable urban waterfront views.
spots.bestTime: Late afternoon or after dark for skyline lights
spots.openingHours: Frequent daily ferry services; check current timetable
spots.topActivities: Star Ferry crossing, Avenue of Stars walk, Symphony of Lights viewing
spots.nearbyAttractions: Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade, Clock Tower, Hong Kong Cultural Centre
spots.travelTips: Take the upper deck at least once and keep an Octopus card ready for simple boarding.
spots.mapQuery: Star Ferry Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

spots.name: Tian Tan Buddha & Po Lin Monastery
spots.location: Ngong Ping, Lantau Island, Hong Kong
spots.shortDescription: A 34-metre seated bronze Buddha above Po Lin Monastery, reached through the green hills of Lantau Island.
spots.whyVisit: The 268-step ascent, monastery setting and Ngong Ping cable-car approach offer a calm contrast to the city.
spots.openingHours: Outdoor monument daily; monastery and cable-car hours vary
spots.entranceFee: Buddha area is free; Ngong Ping 360 and optional attractions are ticketed
spots.topActivities: Ngong Ping 360, Buddha steps, monastery visit, Wisdom Path
spots.nearbyAttractions: Tai O Fishing Village, Lantau hiking trails
spots.travelTips: Book cable-car tickets ahead in busy periods and bring water for the steps.

spots.name: Tai Kwun
spots.shortDescription: A carefully restored former police, magistracy and prison compound transformed into a lively heritage and contemporary-arts destination.
spots.whyVisit: Architecture, exhibitions, cafés and courtyards reveal a layered side of Central beyond the skyscrapers.
spots.openingHours: Daily; gallery and programme hours vary
spots.entranceFee: Grounds are free; some exhibitions or events are ticketed
spots.topActivities: Heritage interpretation, art exhibitions, courtyard dining, photography
spots.travelTips: Check the event calendar before visiting and combine it with a walk through Old Town Central.
spots.mapQuery: Tai Kwun Centre for Heritage and Arts, Hong Kong

spots.name: Temple Street Night Market
spots.shortDescription: A classic Kowloon evening market known for street-food energy, market stalls and old-school Hong Kong atmosphere.
spots.whyVisit: It is a lively place to browse low-cost keepsakes, eat local dishes and experience the city after dark.
spots.topActivities: Street-food dinner, souvenir browsing, market photography
spots.nearbyAttractions: Jade Market, Yau Ma Tei, Kowloon Park
spots.travelTips: Agree on a price before buying and keep valuables secure in the busy lanes.
spots.mapQuery: Temple Street Night Market, Hong Kong

spots.name: Hong Kong Disneyland
spots.location: Penny's Bay, Lantau Island, Hong Kong
spots.shortDescription: A compact Disney park blending classic attractions with Asia-exclusive experiences and a distinctive castle.
spots.whyVisit: It is easy to combine with a Hong Kong stay and has themed merchandise that makes a popular take-home gift.
spots.openingHours: Daily opening hours vary by date
spots.entranceFee: Ticketed; price varies by day and ticket type
spots.topActivities: World of Frozen, parades, night show, themed shopping
spots.travelTips: Reserve tickets and popular dining early; arrive before opening for shorter first-hour queues.

spots.name: Sai Kung & Hong Kong UNESCO Global Geopark
spots.location: Sai Kung, New Territories, Hong Kong
spots.shortDescription: A striking coastal region of volcanic rock, clear water, islands and hiking routes away from the dense urban core.
spots.whyVisit: It shows Hong Kong’s wilder side through boat trips, beaches, seafood and distinctive geological formations.
spots.openingHours: Outdoor destination; boat and visitor-centre hours vary
spots.entranceFee: Free; transport and boat tours cost extra
spots.topActivities: Island boat trip, seafood lunch, hiking, beach time
spots.nearbyAttractions: Sharp Island, High Island Reservoir, Sai Kung Town
spots.travelTips: Check sea conditions before booking a boat and carry sun protection, water and cash.
spots.mapQuery: Hong Kong UNESCO Global Geopark Sai Kung
